---
title: "Analyze Public Goods"
output: html_document
---

This notebook is a lightweight starting point for public goods analysis. It
expects a player-round CSV created by `analysis/scripts/reconstruct_player_round_data.R`
or the Python equivalent.

```{r}
input_path <- "../../sample_data/llm_only/public_goods/player_round.csv"

if (file.exists(input_path)) {
  data <- read.csv(input_path)
  aggregate(action_value ~ round + actor_type, data = data, FUN = mean)
} else {
  message("Sample data not found yet. Generate or copy player-round data before knitting.")
}
```

Remember that players in the same group are not independent observations.
